items.problem: --pr 0 matches every no-PR run because omitempty serializes PR=0 records without a "pr" field, so the loaded records all carry PR=0 and the filter r.PR == pr (pr=0) selects all of them

cross-directory write/read split: review writes via filepath.Join(req.Root,...) where req.Root="." (CWD-relative) while audit-report reads via repoRoot() walk-up — a review launched from a subdirectory of the git root writes records the report will never find

audit write failure is swallowed as Warn and review still exits 0, so a systematically broken compliance ledger (full disk, read-only fs, .atcr/ perm denied) leaves runs reporting success with only a log line that CI rarely surfaces

oversized-line recovery uses unbounded bufio.NewReader.ReadString('\n') which grows the buffer until a newline or EOF — a tampered or corrupted ledger with a multi-GB run-on line OOMs the audit-report command
Append does not fsync, so a crash or power loss between f.Write and Close loses the most recent record — undermining the "tamper-evident compliance ledger" framing in record.go and CHANGELOG
intToStr() is a manual implementation of strconv.Itoa
finding key uses null bytes as separators
RenderReport uses a map for counts without ensuring key existence for canonical columns
